channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Saudi Arabia says it won't establish ties with Israel without creation of Palestinian state
item.description: Saudi Arabia said on Wednesday it would not establish ties with Israel without the creation of a Palestinian state.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration pulling almost all USAID workers off the job worldwide
item.description: The Trump administration says it is pulling almost all U.S. Agency for International Development workers off the job and out of the field worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Colombia's president orders national oil company to cancel US venture over environmental concerns
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — Colombian President Gustavo Petro on Tuesday ordered the state-run oil company Ecopetrol to cancel a joint venture with a U.S.-owned company that was expected to produce around 90,000 barrels of oil per day, citing environmental concerns.
item.dc:creator: MANUEL RUEDA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The Aga Khan, spiritual leader of Ismaili Muslims and a philanthropist, dies at 88
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The Aga Khan, who became the spiritual leader of the world’s millions of Ismaili Muslims at age 20 as a Harvard undergraduate and poured a material empire built on billions of dollars in tithes into building homes, hospitals and schools in developing countries, died Tuesday. He was 88.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk meets with Netanyahu, his son Yair
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk met with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and his son Yair Netanyahu. “So grateful to spend time with these two geniuses,” Yair Netanyahu captioned a Tuesday Instagram post featuring a photo of his father and Musk.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Trump's idea to clear Gaza is a real estate grab: Rep. Auchincloss
item.description: President Trump and his son-in-law view the Gaza Strip as “developable property," a Democratic lawmaker says.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Trump floats US taking over Gaza Strip
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday floated the idea of the U.S. taking over the Gaza Strip, saying America should be responsible for clearing the territory of rubble and unexploded bombs and proposing an “economic development.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: US has frozen some funding for the UN-backed mission to quell gangs in Haiti, UN says
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The U.S. has notified the United Nations that it is freezing some funding to a U.N.-backed mission in Haiti tasked with fighting gangs trying to seize full control of the country’s capital, the U.N. said Tuesday. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: El Salvador’s offer to take in US deportees and violent criminals is unlike any other migrant deal
item.description: SAN SALVADOR, El Salvador (AP) — El Salvador has offered to take in people deported from the U.S. for entering the country illegally and to house some of the country’s violent criminals — even if they’re American citizens. U.S.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO and MARCOS ALEMÁN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump withdraws from 2 UN bodies, orders review of UNESCO
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order withdrawing the U.S. from the United Nations Human Rights Council and from participating in UNRWA, the U.N. agency for Palestinian refugees. Trump also directed the U.S. to review its participation in the U.N.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Trump warns he left instructions to ‘obliterate’ Iran if he is assassinated
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday said that he has left instructions that if Iran were to assassination him, the country should be destroyed.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Making climate-friendly lifestyle choices isn't always easy. India learned the hard way
item.description: A major government initiative to get people to think about their energy choices to pollute less — like cycling instead of driving or using less plastic — has received virtually no funding or publicity in the last year.
item.dc:creator: Sibi Arasu, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Jewish population in West Bank keeps rising. Settlers hope Trump will accelerate growth
item.description: BEIT EL, West Bank (AP) — The Jewish population in the West Bank grew at twice the rate of the general Israeli population last year, according to an advocacy group that hopes the Trump administration will support policies that help accelerate the growth of settlements in the occupied territory.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Congo calls rebels' ceasefire declaration 'false communication' as reports of fighting continue
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Congo's government on Tuesday described the unilateral ceasefire declared by Rwanda-backed rebels in eastern Congo as “false communication,” while the United Nations noted reports of heavy fighting with Congolese forces in the region.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KABUMBA and JEAN-YVES KAMALE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about El Salvador's mega-prison after Trump deal to send people there
item.description: SAN SALVADOR, El Salvador (AP) — The Trump administration and the president of El Salvador said Monday that they'd struck a deal allowing the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARCOS ALEMÁN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Greek island Santorini evacuated after earthquakes shake tourist destination
item.description: Hundreds of earthquakes that have rattled the Greek islands since last week have prompted the evacuation of thousands from the tourist hotspot of Santorini.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Greenland looks to ban foreign political donations
item.description: Greenland’s Parliament on Tuesday debated whether it would ban political donations from foreign or anonymous contributors.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: As hundreds of quakes rattle Greek islands, a few brave tourists have Santorini to themselves
item.description: SANTORINI, Greece (AP) — The hundreds of earthquakes that have rattled Greek islands on the Aegean Sea are a “seismic swarm" and could continue for weeks before eventually diminishing, the Santorini mayor told The Associated Press on Tuesday — pausing to chuckle as new tremors shook items on his des
item.dc:creator: DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US aid freeze puts at risk Ukraine’s wartime help for frontline evacuees
item.description: PAVLOHRAD, Ukraine (AP) — In what used to be the concert hall in this town in eastern Ukraine, cots are arranged on stage. Instead of music, the room is filled with the muffled sobs of local people driven from their homes by fighting in the country’s almost three-year war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: First military flight lands in Guantanamo Bay with migrants deported from the US
item.description: The first U.S. military flight deporting migrants from the United States to Guantanamo Bay has landed in Cuba.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp And Lolita C. Baldor, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Denmark PM repeats Greenland ‘not for sale,’ but would welcome more American troops on Arctic island
item.description: Danish Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen said she would welcome President Donald Trump sending more U.S. troops to Greenland, which is "not for sale.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China hits US with retaliatory tariffs
item.description: China hit the U.S. back with retaliatory tariffs across the board over President Trump’s enforcement of tariffs on Chinese goods that went into effect on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: PM declares 'worst mass shooting in Swedish history' after gunman kills 10 on school campus
item.description: Swedish police are responding to a school shooting in Orebro, Sweden, on Tuesday after reports said at least 10 people were killed
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Trump suggests Palestinians leave Gaza as talks on ceasefire resume
item.description: President Donald Trump has suggested that displaced Palestinians be permanently resettled outside Gaza and proposed the U.S. take “ownership” in redeveloping the area.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: El Salvador agrees to accept US deportees of any nationality following meeting with Rubio
item.description: El Salvador's President Nayib Bukele has offered to accept convicted criminals of any nationality the U.S. wants to deport and house them in his country's prison system.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Modi's ruling party looks to topple corruption crusader in Delhi poll with graft allegations
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — The resurgent Hindu nationalist party of India's Prime Minister Narendra Modi is eyeing a state legislature election in Delhi where it is looking to unseat the governing movement which has led the Indian capital for over a decade but has recently been embroiled in graft allegations.
item.dc:creator: ASHOK SHARMA and SHEIKH SAALIQ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A Russia-like crackdown has jailed dozens in Georgia, with human rights groups sounding the alarm
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Jailed journalist Mzia Amaghlobeli gets weaker every day as her hunger strike has reached three weeks in Rustavi, a town near the Georgian capital of Tbilisi, her lawyer says.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE and DASHA LITVINOVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump won't rule out deploying US troops to support rebuilding Gaza, sees 'long-term' US ownership
item.description: President Donald Trump has suggested that displaced Palestinians in Gaza be permanently resettled outside the war-torn territory and proposed the U.S. take “ownership” in redeveloping the area.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani And Tia Goldenberg, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mexican cartels targeting Border Patrol agents with kamikaze drones, explosives amid Trump crackdown: report
item.description: Mexican drug cartels have planned attacks on U.S. Border Patrol agents with kamikaze drones and other explosives to combat a crackdown by the Trump administration at the southern border.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump orders creation of US sovereign wealth fund, says it could own part of TikTok
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday signed an executive order directing the U.S. to take steps to start developing a government-owned investment fund that he said could be used to profit off of TikTok if he's successful at finding it an American buyer.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Another car bomb in a northern Syria city kills at least 19, mostly women
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Another car bomb exploded on the outskirts of a city in northern Syria on Monday, killing at least 19 people and leaving over a dozen wounded, hospital workers and emergency responders said.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and OMAR ALBAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A blast in Moscow kills 2, including the founder of a unit fighting in eastern Ukraine, reports say
item.description: An explosion rocked an upscale residential block in Moscow early Monday, killing two people – including the founder of a battalion that battled Kyiv’s forces in eastern Ukraine -- and injuring three others, Russian authorities and news reports said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Africa hits back at Trump’s claim that it is ‘confiscating land,' as US aid to country threatened
item.description: The South African president has hit back at President Donald Trump for claiming the government is "confiscating" land. President Trump also claimed certain classes of people are being treated ‘very badly.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US-backed commander says his Kurdish-led group wants a secular and civil state in post-Assad Syria
item.description: HASSAKEH, Syria (AP) — The commander of the main U.S.-backed force in Syria said Sunday the recent ouster of the Assad family from power should be followed by building a secular, civil and decentralized state that treats all its citizens equally no matter their religion or ethnicity.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Arab nations reject Trump’s suggestion to relocate Palestinians from Gaza to Egypt and Jordan
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Powerful Arab nations on Saturday rejected U.S. President Donald Trump’s suggestion to relocate Palestinians from Gaza to neighboring Egypt and Jordan.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas releases 3 more hostages, including US citizen, as part of fragile ceasefire deal
item.description: Hamas on Saturday freed three more hostages, including American-Israeli Keith Siegel, in the latest phase of a fragile ceasefire with Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Hostage releases begin in Gaza Strip
item.description: Hamas handed captive Israeli soldier Agam Berger over to the Red Cross, the first of eight hostages set to be released today.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation
